Copy blueprint - Week 2 – Session 4
Housekeeping
Put the link to the niche in the Facebook group
In copypro fill out the entire niche so (this takes time)
3:00
Homework session critiques:
1. 	Look for primary complaints
2. 	Looking for secondary complaints
3. 	Look for avatars
Good copy needs both primary complaints and secondary complaints (most copy misses this
and it causes problems)
Make sure the pronouns are correct you vs their when writing to groups.
The copy needs to resonate with the avatar being targeted
8:30
Look for “the one thing” that narrows down the problem for the avatar. Put all of the symptoms
into a category of one named idea. (Its easier to solve one problem for a person than many)
Break up sentences and thoughts for “read flow” the formatting matters
11:30
Long form stories are good for sales pages – trim the story for an email
Take a long story and put it into a 500 word email (story trimming)
Take a 500 word email and cut it into 200 words (test the 3)
· 	I went on a journey and it led me to __________, then ________ led me to _________ . I
found _________ and its called __________ . You can find out more here at __________ (link)
· 	Leave hints instead of long content when cutting back to 200 words (here is what
happened … click here to see the rest of the story)
· 	Here is something that happened _________ and this is why it was surprising
__________
· 	Write a concise story
15:30 – Gym owners
This is a tier 2 self identifying avatar ( a tier 3 isnt needed for this example)
Cross fit people are called “crossfitter” – this is teir 3
“Fitness business commander” may not get the attention needed for a tier 3 avatar. Try to keep
the name to 1 or 2 words. Use the example box on the right of the screen in copy pro to help
keep the naming consistent. Short names are better. “fitness pro” may be best. A tier 3 may not
be needed.
“Today we are going to talk about ________” as one overarching idea may be too much as one
topic. If the idea is repetitive without being broken up;
· 	Today we are going to talk about weight loss
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

· 	Today we are going to talk about weight loss
· 	Today we are going to talk about weight loss
Is not the same as:
· 	Today we are going to talk about thighs
· 	Today we are going to to talk about shoulders
· 	Today we are going to talk about sculpting
Take a big subject and break it up into sub categories to keep it interesting for the reader and so
they cant always guess where the conversation is going to go. Consistently being predicable is
boring.
21:00
Pronouns may not be need in copypro – pay attention to how the paragraph on the side bar works
out.
The challenges happen in the secondary complaints.
Fitness domination – sounds like a club
Something that gets attention easily will get people to read more.
23:00
Cut down words in the primary complaint and still make the copy sound relevant to the avatar –
The less words the better.
Secondary compliments – Get used to trimming the content in copypro
Check the pronouns
Taking the time to trim the content back is worth its weight in gold.
Trim as many words as possible from the secondary complaints. Learn how to remove words and
make the copy more succinct.
Word alchemy
Low cash flow = dreadful cash flow, cash flow hell, lousy cash flow
Get the idea to stick out in the mind of the reader and make it visceral.
Low cash flow may also be a primary complaint
Decline of leads can be modified – check the example box on the side bar of copypro
28:00
Speed writing tips – 10 ways to write faster
This isnt typing speed – the time it takes to write the copy – this can be spoken and then
transcribed. Editing will need to be done after the stream of consciousness.
Learn how to be a in flow state of creativity – I am not the writer be the expert at dictation.
Not in the course – learn how to get into flow
1. 	Step 1 - 10 minutes of silence – no phones – no internet (take a cold shower)
2. 	Step 2 - Feel the 3 modes:
There is the real me (I write as me)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

Write as me but not me (I can write as a Dr but I need to be the character of the dr) No Drama,
trouble or neurosis.
Avatar – Write to one person not a group (having a picture of this avatar helps)
Book: Alter ego effect – Todd Herman may help to understand this avatar – not in class my
notes
If handcuffs are on me mentally I cant write effectively
3. 	Step 4 – I am not writing as me. I am writing as my ideal character and self. I am free
from restraints.
4. 	Step 4 – Write for 50 minutes without interruptions for a sales letter. For email set a
timer for 10 minutes. Do not edit. Do not think about formulas, structure or research. When in
character mode the writing will be freeing from ego. This is the being looking at me while
writing
5. 	Step 5 – limit myself to 4 sets. Write for 50 minutes rest for 10. Do not break character
6. 	Step 6 – After 50 minutes read to the avatars picture. If every words sounds like it is read
to that avatar then the writing is on the right track (editing comes later and it isnt needed to be
worried about right now)
7. 	Step 7 – Step away for a full day – leave the copy alone for 24 hours.
8. 	Step 8 – Read the letter into a recorder and say it to the avatar. If it doesn’t feel or sound
like something that would be said to the avatar cut it.
9. 	Step 9 –Edit – trim words (this is hard work). Cut out every word that isnt needed while
maintaining the meaning of the sentience. Remove repetitive words that will not make sense.
People know who they are (struggling to pay bills)
10. Step 10 – Publish – fearlessly present the work to the client.
38:30
Litigator mindset
2 kick ass writers – Secondary goals are used to build a case
Swiped.co/file/from-your-knees-golf-ad-by-john-carlton/
· 	Slaughtering – good word choice (I don’t have to be a golfer to see this visually)
· 	He uses the power of “one thing”
· 	Stink at your game right now – part of avatar vernacular
· 	Instantly uncorks – Good choice of verbs
· 	From your knees …
· 	Doesn’t matter if you are totally out of shape (secondary complaints) – switch the phrases
to are you … totally out of shape
· 	You don’t have to hit tee shots… but you could
· 	Think of the bets you could win…
· 	If you are like me (empathy)
· 	Stiff back – arthritis
· 	Duality (good / bad)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

· 	Rapport story
· 	Secondary complaints (what else is the customer bitching about that isnt really talked
about)
· 	Words are bolded on purpose
· 	Are you x, are you y are you z … these are the reasons why you cant do primary
complaint (what are the other problems the avatar has besides the primary complaint.
46:00
Secondary complaint formula
How to find secondary complaints – magical question
Avatar: I have a problem with _________ ? (Primary complaint – 30,000 view)
Expert: “Why do you think that is”
Avatar: Answer ________ (secondary complaint – real view)
“Why do you think that is” has he ability to answer every secondary complaint there
is
48:00
Homeowner assignment
Go to copy pro and search for primary complaint
Go to email solo “secondary complaint” there are 32 of them. Pick several and look for one I
like. Notice how they fit in. Trim the primary complaint and secondary complaint for an email.
This words in emails, ads and sales pages.
I cant hit my t shot – why do you think that is – Answer ________ (secondary complain)
49:00
2nd example:
Swiped.com/file/skinny-doctor-ad-by-green-tree-press/
When my skinny doctor laughed at me I actually threw my dress at him… (Jon thinks this is
one of the best headlines written)
It sounds like she threw the dress with emotions
When a person is fat – women like to stand near them to look better.
Rodney Dangerfield – If you want to look thin stand next to fat people
This is visceral;
Primary complaint
· 	I cant lose weight
· 	I feel fat
The copywriter started with the secondary complaint:
· 	I was too tired
· 	I was embarrassed
· 	I waddled when I walked
· 	I sweat when I ate
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 5 --

Rapport + empathy is used in this advertisement
Why do you think you are fat only works in the litigator formula
Reverse formula
· 	What’s the number one thing that bothers you about …
· 	What else is hurting you?
· 	Describe the rest of your pain
The avatar needs to be understood in order to see primary complaints and secondary complaints
This is in your face writing that is empathetic
Lack of optimism on the part of the voice of the author – this message is written to someone who
doesn’t have hope and is worried they will never fit in.
The 4 persuasion quadrants are used in this advertisement:
· 	Wants
· 	Needs
· 	Fears
· 	Empathy
Look for the feelings of the avatar
The author is writing as the character – he couldn’t do this if he didn’t know what was going on
in the mind of the avatar
55:45
Primary goals and secondary goals also need to be addressed in the copy (week 4)
The copy started off with complaints – this is the first thing out of their mouth when they talk
about their problem. I also need to recognize #2, #3, #4, #5 and so on that they are not willing to
talk about.
· 	Tired
· 	Embarrassed
· 	Waddle when walking
57:00
Questions and answers:
When does speed writing work vs long writing – Jon speed wrights all of the time. He uses a
timer most of the time.
Creating an avatar – high performance entrepreneur is a good tier 2 avatar. This isnt a tier 3. Tier
1 sucks. Tier 2 is needed. Tier 3 is extra credit
What is the best way to move homework copy from copypro to email format? Search – email
solo (pick a few – start a project – choose niche) werewolves – copy and paste the url slug into
Facebook.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
